top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
68 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 68 miles.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
62ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,200ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: November in Loutraki, Peloponnese, Greece can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki’s 68 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 68 miles of mixed terrain in Loutraki, Peloponnese, Greece.
